mnemonics.title: Email Components
mnemonics.content: MUA sends to MTA, MTA talks MTA, MDA delivers - Remember: MUAs Make Tasty Donuts, MTA MDA MUA MRA - Mail Users Transfer And Deliver Messages Actively

mnemonics.title: SMTP Commands
mnemonics.content: H-M-R-D-Q: HELO/EHLO → MAIL FROM → RCPT TO → DATA → QUIT. Mnemonic: Happy Mice Run Deep Quickly

mnemonics.title: Email Headers Priority
mnemonics.content: FROGS: From, Received, Organization, GS (Subject - Generic Subject), Date - Actually: From, To, Reply-To, Received, Date, Subject, CC, BCC, Message-ID

acronyms.usage: Protocol for sending emails between servers
Protocol for retrieving emails from server (downloads and deletes)
Protocol for accessing emails on server (keeps on server)
Extension to support multimedia in emails
Email client like Outlook, Thunderbird, Gmail
Server component that delivers to mailbox
Standard for email encryption and digital signatures

visualTips.visualization: Draw a horizontal line with boxes: Sender MUA → MRA → MTA → Internet → MTA → MDA → Recipient MRA → Recipient MUA. Label arrows with protocols: SMTP for transfer, POP3/IMAP for retrieval.
Draw a flow: Connection Established → (EHLO/HELO) → Mail Transaction (MAIL, RCPT, DATA) → Connection Closed. Each state shows valid commands and transitions.
Show RFC 822 format: [Headers Block] → blank line → [Body Block]. Headers as key-value pairs, Body as content. For MIME: show Content-Type headers and encoded body sections.
Draw nested boxes: message/mixed → [text/plain part] + [image/jpeg attachment] + [application/pdf attachment]. Show boundary markers between parts.

analogies.analogy: MUA is like your post office counter (where you write/send letters), MTA is like mail trucks transporting letters between cities, MDA is like the postal carrier delivering to your mailbox. SMTP is the set of rules for how postal workers hand over letters.

POP3 is like buying a newspaper - you take it home and the newsstand doesn't keep a copy. IMAP is like reading a newspaper online - the same content is available on your phone, tablet, and computer, all synchronized.
MIME encoding binary files for email is like converting a physical photo into text characters (like in spy movies) so it can be sent as a text telegram, then converting it back at the destination.
Email encryption with public-key is like a locked mailbox where everyone knows the slot (public key) to drop letters, but only the person with the unique key (private key) can open and read them.
